findings.description: Slide 3 (History of Hydrilla at Lake Tillery) has a two-column layout in the PDF with the site-acreage table on the left and the TGC stocking table on the right. In the HTML, these are rendered as sequential single-column content, losing the side-by-side arrangement.
findings.evidence: PDF shows two tables side by side on the same slide. HTML renders them stacked vertically.

findings.description: Slide 5 (Hydrilla Management in 2023) has a two-column layout with bullet points on the left and an aerial map on the right. The HTML converts this to a single-column stacked layout.
findings.evidence: PDF shows text and map side by side. HTML shows text above map sequentially.

findings.description: Slide 6 (Proposed Management Activities for 2024) has a three-panel layout with text on the left and two maps on the right. The HTML collapses this into a single-column layout.
findings.evidence: PDF shows text alongside two aerial treatment maps. HTML stacks all content vertically.

findings.description: Slide 7 (Fall Vegetation Surveys) has a two-column layout with bullet points on the left and a photo on the right. HTML renders as single column.
findings.evidence: PDF shows text and rake-toss photo side by side. HTML stacks them vertically.

findings.description: Slide 9 has two images side by side (Biobase heatmap on left, SONAR screenshot on right). HTML stacks them vertically.
findings.evidence: PDF shows two images in a two-column arrangement. HTML shows them stacked.

findings.description: Slide 10 has two maps side by side (Hydrilla coverage map on left, density rating map on right). HTML stacks them vertically.
findings.evidence: PDF shows two GIS maps in side-by-side layout. HTML renders them sequentially stacked.

findings.description: Slide 2 (History of Hydrilla at Lake Tillery) - the lake/dock photograph is present in both versions but positioned differently relative to the text in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows photo on right side of slide next to bullet points. HTML shows it below the bullet text content.

findings.description: Slide 8 - the satellite map showing survey points across Lake Tillery appears in the PDF as a large central image, but in the HTML it appears to be cut off or only partially visible.
findings.evidence: PDF shows full lake survey point map. HTML rendering shows the map but appears truncated at the bottom.

findings.description: Fabricated URL in the AWCP Website link. The HTML contains a long URL with additional text fragment that does not appear in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'AWCP Website (Aquatic Weed Control Program | NC DEQ)' with no full URL visible. HTML fabricates a full URL: 'https://www.deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/water-resources/water-planning/water-supply-planning/aquatic-weed-control-program#:%7E:text=Purpose%20The%20purpose%20of%20the%20Aquatic%20Weed%20Control...' - the fragment identifier with text highlight was not in the source PDF.

findings.description: Multiple h2 headings use the same text 'History of Hydrilla at Lake Tillery' across pages 2, 3, and 4, which mirrors the PDF but may cause structural confusion. More importantly, the document title h1 is 'Hydrilla management at Lake Tillery, past and present' which is correct, but there is a nested <main> element creating a duplicated structural wrapper.
findings.evidence: HTML has <main> nested inside <main>, which is invalid HTML structure, though the heading text itself matches the PDF.

findings.description: The page header/department name 'Department of Environmental Quality' appears redundantly in the header section and is also preserved in page footer divs throughout, but this mirrors the PDF's repeated branding on each slide.
findings.evidence: The header contains 'Department of Environmental Quality' and each section footer contains it as well, matching the PDF slide format.

findings.description: Some alt text descriptions are reasonable approximations but cannot be fully verified against actual image content. The alt text for the page 1 header image describes 'a lighthouse at sunset' which appears consistent with the PDF imagery.
findings.evidence: Alt text like 'Background photo of a lighthouse at sunset' for page 1, 'Photo of Swift Island boat ramp' for page 2 image - these appear reasonable given PDF context.

findings.description: The AWCP Website link text in the PDF shows only 'Aquatic Weed Control Program | NC DEQ' as a hyperlink label. The HTML constructs a full URL href that includes a Google-style text fragment highlight, which was not visible in the PDF source.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Aquatic Weed Control Program | NC DEQ' as link text. HTML href includes '#:%7E:text=Purpose%20The%20purpose%20of%20the%20Aquatic%20Weed%20Control...' fragment not shown in PDF.

findings.description: The PDF page 2 contains the typo 'they Yadkin' and the HTML preserves this typo faithfully, which is correct behavior.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML: 'First time it was reported in they Yadkin – Pee Dee River basin.'
